text.title: Data Analysis Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every data-analysis cycle enforces.

rule.statement: Every field row carries: name, type (one of canonical types: string, int, float, bool, date, datetime, uuid, json, blob), source_system, and a citation (table + column or file + line).

rationale.source: data modelling / Kimball, The Data Warehouse Toolkit (2013)

rule.rationale: Untyped or unsourced fields produce silent ETL bugs and reconciliation gaps across systems.

rule.statement: Quality baseline scores each entity on six dimensions: accuracy, completeness, consistency, timeliness, validity, uniqueness — each 0-100 with measurement method.
rule.rationale: Without standardized DQ scoring, "data is bad" remains anecdote. Six-dimension scoring exposes which lever to pull.

rule.statement: Business rules expressed as machine-checkable predicates (Python / SQL / OCL) — not prose. Each predicate names the entity, the condition, and the violation severity.

rationale.source: rule-driven development / Date, What Not How (2000)

rule.rationale: Prose rules drift from implementation. Predicates are testable; the validator runs them.

rule.statement: Every entity in the dictionary has a NAMED owner (full name + role) — not "Data team". Owner approves field additions, type changes, and DQ baseline shifts.
rule.rationale: Anonymous ownership produces zombie tables nobody maintains and silent quality decay.

rule.statement: Dictionary carries a semver version_tag; every committed change bumps minor (additive) or major (breaking). Downstream consumers pin a version range.

rationale.source: schema-evolution / Confluent Schema Registry best practices

rule.rationale: Unpinned dictionaries break consumers on every drift. Semver lets consumers opt into changes deliberately.

example.title: Field row satisfying r1
example.code: | name | type | source_system | source_ref | nullable | pii | |-------------|----------|---------------|---------------------|----------|-----| | customer_id | uuid | crm | crm.customers.id | false | no | | birth_date | date | crm | crm.customers.dob | true | yes |
example.note: Typed, source-cited, nullability + PII flags present.

example.title: Field row failing r1
example.code: | name | type | |-------------|----------| | cust_id | id | | birth | unknown |
example.note: "id" and "unknown" are not canonical types; source missing.
